html:
RECOUP Integrative Root-cause Rehabilitation Program for Multiple Sclerosis

Multiple Sclerosis (MS) is a chronic, immune-mediated neurodegenerative disorder that affects the central nervous system (CNS), particularly the brain and spinal cord. It leads to demyelination and neuronal loss, resulting in a wide range of neurological symptoms including motor dysfunction, sensory loss, fatigue, spasticity, cognitive deficits, and visual disturbances.

According to the Atlas of MS (2020), more than 2.8 million people globally are affected by MS. An estimated 1.5 to 2 lakh individuals (11 people per 100,000) in India live with MS and its prevalence is increasing. It is most commonly diagnosed between the ages of 20 and 40, and women are affected at twice the rate of men. MS is now increasingly recognized as a disease involving not just autoimmunity and neuroinflammation, but also gut dysbiosis, mitochondrial dysfunction, metabolic imbalance, and environmental toxicity.

Conventional treatments focus on immunosuppression and symptom control but often fail to address the root causes. Emerging evidence supports an integrative, personalized, and root-cause-based approach that utilizes functional or root-cause medicine and neurorehabilitation strategies to halt or even reverse disease progression.

MS pathophysiology involves a complex interplay of:

  • Immune dysregulation: Aberrant activation of T-cells and microglia.
  • Mitochondrial dysfunction: Energy failure in neurons and oligodendrocytes.
  • Gut-brain axis disruption: Increased intestinal permeability, dysbiosis, molecular mimicry.
  • Environmental toxicity: Heavy metals, mold, EMF, pesticides, microplastics.
  • Nutritional deficiencies: B12, D, magnesium, omega-3 fatty acids, and polyphenols.
  • Neuroinflammation: Cytokine storm, oxidative stress.
  • Lifestyle triggers: Sedentary behavior, chronic stress, poor sleep.

Recoup's Integrative, Root-Cause Rehabilitation approach addresses these elements systematically using our 4R protocol: Relief of symptoms, Root Cause Redressal, Restoration, and Regeneration.

R 1: Relief of Symptoms

The acute presentation of MS can include fatigue, spasticity, weakness, dizziness, visual disturbances, neuropathic pain, bladder/bowel dysfunction, and anxiety. This phase is characterized by the need for symptom stabilization, the provision of immediate supportive measures, and the promotion of patient autonomy.

Suggested Interventions during this phase of treatment include:

  1. Neuro-education: Teaching MS patients about the disease process, neuroplasticity, and empowerment through self-care.
  2. Physical Therapy: Spasticity management, proprioception (Proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation/PNF), balance retraining, Gait retraining, fall prevention, coordination exercises, posture control,
  3. Aquatic Therapy: Buoyancy-assisted movement, pain control, gait training
  4. Whole Body Vibration Therapy: For muscle activation and balance.
  5. Body Weight Supported Treadmill Training: to improve gait and balance.
  6. Hippotherapy (on a horse simulator) to improve balance.
  7. Tai Chi and Yoga: Improve proprioception, balance, breathing, and calmness.
  8. Franklin Method: Neurocognitive imagery for retraining movement patterns.
  9. Occupational Therapy (OT): ADL re-training: grooming, dressing, feeding, Energy conservation strategies, Fine motor training, Adaptive equipment training.
  10. Exergaming with Smart Palyball: For improving physical functions (balance, upper extremity function) and cognitive functions (attention and visuospatial skills), as well as enhancing motivation and participation in daily activities.
  11. Sleep Optimization: Using nutraceuticals, sleep coaching, iCBT, and iACT.
  12. Colon Hydrotherapy: For gut detoxification and bowel regulation.
  13. Anti-inflammatory Nutrition (Wahls Level 1)
  14. Psychological First Aid: CBT-based approaches for adjustment, Pain Reprocessing Therapy, Emotional regulation coaching

R 2: Root Cause Redressal

Focuses on investigating and addressing the upstream biochemical, immunological, microbial, and environmental contributors to MS pathology.

Suggested interventions for this phase include:

  1. Comprehensive Functional Testing: Gut, Immunity, Toxin, and Nutrient panels
  2. Colon Hydrotherapy
  3. Red Light Therapy (Far and Near Infrared)
  4. Cold and heat exposure (sauna, contrast showers)
  5. Breathwork and vagal nerve stimulation
  6. Exercise Therapy with HRV biofeedback
  7. Wahls Paleo Plus Diet
  8. Mitochondrial Enhancement: IV Nutrition
  9. Mind-Body Therapies: EMDR, DBT, EFT for trauma and stress modulation.
  10. Guided Imagery
  11. Ayurveda: Panchakarma Detoxification

R 3: Restoring physiological balances

Aims to reintegrate biological systems, restore resilience, and enhance cognition and movement quality. Interventions include:

  1. Advanced Neurorehabilitation: Coordination Dynamics Therapy (CDT), Virtual and Augmented Reality-Based Therapy, Functional Electrical Stimulation, Neurofeedback, and HRV-based breath retraining.
  2. Alexander Technique and Feldenkrais: Enhancing Body Awareness and Deep Sensorimotor Retraining.
  3. CGM-based program for insulin sensitivity and dysglycemia.
  4. IV Micronutrient Therapy: Glutathione, Vitamin C, magnesium, B vitamins, amino acids.
  5. Treatment of Toxins and Chronic Infections (if needed): using detoxification, chelation, etc.
  6. Occupational Therapy and Ergonomic Support: Home modifications include handrails, voice-activated lighting, assistive devices, and Fall risk reduction plans. Workstation assessment and ergonomic modifications.
  7. Hormone Profile Review and HRT (if needed).
  8. Psychological Empowerment: Purpose therapy, Positive psychology journaling, Group resilience training.
  9. Positive Neuroplasticity Training (PNT) is a program designed to help individuals with MS build new neural pathways that enhance positive emotions, resilience, and overall well-being. By focusing on positive experiences and practicing techniques to "take in the good," PNT helps participants rewire their brains to prioritize positive emotions and cultivate lasting inner strengths.

R 4: Regeneration and Longevity

Focuses on promoting neuroregeneration, reducing biological age, and building long-term resilience against progression and relapse. Interventions include:

  1. Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy (HBOT) enhances stem cell activity and oxygenation in areas of demyelination.
  2. Ozone Therapy and Molecular Hydrogen: Immune Modulation and Oxidative Stress Reduction.
  3. IV Nutrition
  4. Peptide Therapies involve using specific peptides to modulate the immune response, particularly by targeting myelin-reactive T cells. This experimental approach aims to reduce inflammation and damage, induce bystander suppression, or promote remyelination.
  5. Autophagy Enhancement: Intermittent fasting, Polyphenol support
  6. Anti-Aging Interventions: Telomere-preserving diets.
  7. Frailty Index Monitoring: HRV, VO2 max, grip strength, brain endurance.
  8. Relapse Prevention Planning: Personalized flare-up action plan, Regular follow-up via Recoup EPHR, Mental preparedness, and crisis strategy coaching

The RECOUP 4R Program for MS offers a clinically comprehensive, integrative, and data-driven approach to support MS patients on their journey toward healing, resilience, and independence. By combining neuroscience, regenerative therapies, functional nutrition, and mind-body medicine, we empower individuals to move beyond symptom control and toward neurorestoration and lifelong wellness.

RECOUP's Credentials

  • Dr Deepak Sharan has over 25 years of experience treating MS.
  • He is a Consultant in Orthopaedic Surgery, Rehabilitation, Orthopaedic Rheumatology, Pain, Musculoskeletal, Regenerative, Functional and Lifestyle Medicine, Ergonomics, Occupational Health.
  • Diplomate of the International Board of Lifestyle Medicine (Dip IBLM).
  • Certified Functional Medicine Physician from the Institute for Functional Medicine, USA, and the Functional Medicine Academy, USA. Trained in the Wahl's Protocol by Dr Terry Wahls.
  • Certificate in Anti-Ageing and Precision Medicine (The American Academy of Anti-Aging Medicine).
  • Advanced courses in Functional Medicine, Kharrazian Institute, USA - Neuroinflammation, Neurodegeneration, Gastrointestinal, Autoimmunity, Cognitive Decline and Dementia, Chronic Fatigue and Pain Syndromes, Mood and Anxiety Disorders, Longevity and Wellness Optimisation.
  • Other RECOUP team members are trained by him.
